title: The God Who Wanted to Die
subject: Jergal, the original god of death in the Forgotten Realms who willingly surrendered his divine portfolio to the Dead Three
hook: A god so weary of his own existence he gave away his divinity to three mortals just to feel something

title: Why Mind Flayers Eat Brains
subject: The true origin and biology of Illithids, their elder brain dependency, tadpole lifecycle, and why they harvest humanoid intellect
hook: The reason is more horrifying than hunger — it is memory theft on a cosmic scale

title: The Place Even Gods Fear
subject: The Far Realm — what it is, what lives there, why it exists outside the Great Wheel cosmology, and what happens to those who peer into it
hook: It is not a plane. It is not a dimension. It is something the multiverse was never meant to touch

title: The Vampire Who Betrayed a God
subject: Cazador Szarr and the dark history of vampirism in the Forgotten Realms, tracing back to Bhaal and the original curse
hook: Vampirism was not always a curse. Once, it was a reward

title: The True Horror of Liches
subject: What a lich actually is, the phylactery process, what they sacrifice to achieve undeath, and the most powerful liches in D&D lore
hook: To become a lich you must first murder your own soul — and that is only the beginning

title: Vecna Before He Became a God
subject: The full origin story of Vecna — from orphaned mortal to archmage to lich to deity, and what he actually wants
hook: He was not born evil. He was made into something worse

title: The City Built on a Dead God
subject: Sigil and the Lady of Pain — the city at the center of the multiverse, who rules it, and why no god can enter
hook: There is a city floating in the void where the gods themselves are banned on pain of death

title: What Dragons Actually Are
subject: The true cosmological origin of dragons in D&D — Io, the dragon god, Bahamut and Tiamat as his split aspects, and why dragons hoard
hook: Dragons are not monsters. They are fragments of a murdered god

title: The Drow Who Defied Their Goddess
subject: The history of the drow, Lolth's betrayal of the elven pantheon, and what the Underdark really is
hook: The dark elves were not always dark. A single goddess poisoned an entire race

title: The Forgotten Realm Nobody Talks About
subject: The Shadowfell — its origin, its relationship to the Material Plane, the Raven Queen, and what happens to souls that get lost there
hook: Every location on the Material Plane has a twisted reflection. Most people never realize they are being watched from it

title: The War That Broke the Heavens
subject: The Dawn War between the gods and the primordials — the founding conflict of the D&D cosmology that shaped everything
hook: Before mortals existed, gods and titans tore the universe apart. The scars are still there

title: What Happens When You Die in D&D
subject: The actual cosmological journey of a soul after death — the Fugue Plane, the gods claiming souls, the Wall of the Faithless, and what happens to those nobody claims
hook: Death in the D&D multiverse is not the end. For some souls, it is something far worse

title: The Demon Lord Who Was Once an Angel
subject: Zariel's fall from celestial to Archduke of Avernus — one of the most tragic lore arcs in D&D
hook: She volunteered to fight demons. The demons won

title: The Monster That Cannot Be Killed
subject: The Tarrasque — what it actually is, its origin theories, why the gods won't destroy it, and what would really happen if it woke
hook: The gods could destroy it. They choose not to. The reason is more unsettling than the monster itself

title: The Nine Hells Are Not What You Think
subject: The true structure of Baator, the Blood War, why devils corrupt souls rather than destroy them, and the economy of the Nine Hells
hook: Hell is not a place of punishment. It is a machine — and souls are the fuel

title: The Elves Who Became Monsters
subject: The origin of the Yuan-Ti — once a human civilization that sacrificed their humanity to serpent gods for power
hook: They were not born as snake people. They chose to become them

title: The Spell That Destroyed a World
subject: The Spellplague — what caused it, what Mystra's death meant for the Forgotten Realms, and the century of chaos that followed
hook: Magic is not a tool. It is a living force. And when it died, the world broke

title: The God of Murder's Secret Plan
subject: Bhaal's scheme to return from death through the Bhaalspawn — why he seeded mortal children with his essence and what he ultimately wanted
hook: He was killed by mortals. So he hid his soul inside mortal children and waited

title: What Aberrations Actually Are
subject: The cosmological origin of aberrations — creatures from the Far Realm that broke into the multiverse and became the mind flayers, beholders, and worse
hook: They do not belong here. They were never meant to exist in this reality. And yet here they are

title: The Underdark Has No Bottom
subject: The true depth and scope of the Underdark — its layers, what lives in the deepest regions, and what ancient civilizations are buried there
hook: People think they know what lives underground. They have not dug deep enough
